title: quercus dentata subsp. stewardii
intro: Quercus dentata subsp. stewardii, also known as the Japanese oak or Asian evergreen oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree in the beech family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Quercus dentata subsp. stewardii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to reap its medicinal benefits.
